# Regular passenger flights between US and Venezuela restart this week

[![Picture of Sebastián Polito](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/0bede48640b9e23d4dca6b96c65a8004?s=300&d=mm&r=g)](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/author/sebastian/)

[Sebastián Polito](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/author/sebastian/)

- Updated: April 30, 2026

[Add us as a preferred source](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=https://www.flightradar24.com)

A **historic milestone will take place for Venezuela on April 30th,** as it will **resume regular flights with the United States** after a **seven-year hiatus.**

[![](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Caracas-CCS-2004.jpg)](https://www.jetphotos.com/photo/6425272) Caracas – December 2004

**Following Nicolás Maduro’s capture** by the **United States,** certain **restrictions on Venezuela’s aviation sector have been lifted, allowing the resumption of passenger and cargo flights to/from the United States. Some restrictions are still in place, like the ban on Argentine airlines flying over the country**.

During **April**, **some U.S. and Canadian airlines,** including **SkyLease Cargo and Cargojet Airways,** operated **cargo flights to Caracas** for the first time in **several years**. As for other Venezuelan cities, **Amerijet International conducted a special flight between Miami and Valencia** using a **Boeing 767-300(F)** on **April 26th**.

[![](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Porlamar-2025.jpg)](https://www.jetphotos.com/photo/11791871) Porlamar

# American is back

On **April 30th at 14:55 local time,** the **first American Airlines flight in seven years will land at Maiquetía International Airport** (CCS/SVMI), located 30 kilometers north of Caracas. The **airline will initially offer a daily frequency** operated by **Envoy Air’s Embraer 175 aircraft,** which are **configured for 76 passengers** in **two classes**( **12 seats in Business** and **64 seats in Economy**).

**American will be the first U.S. airline** to **restore flights between the United States and Venezuela,** as well as the **first airline from any country to do so.** In addition, **it will be the first to expand its operations in this market,** as it has already **confirmed that it will double its flight frequency on the Miami-Caracas** route starting **May 21st**.

With its initial service, **American will provide 532 weekly seats between Miami and Caracas and vice versa,** a **number significantly lower than the airline’s peak in Venezuela,** when it **operated flights to the Venezuelan capital from Dallas, Fort Lauderdale, Miami, New York,** and **San Juan** (Puerto Rico), as well as between **Miami and Maracaibo**, totaling around **10 daily flights.**

[![](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/American-CCS-2016.jpg)](https://www.jetphotos.com/photo/8329013) Landing in Caracas – July 2016

### Schedule between Miami and Caracas · American

Daily · eff **April 30th**

- AA 3599  [Miami MIA](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/mia) 10:16 · 13:36 [CCS Caracas](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/ccs)
- AA 4194  [Caracas CCS](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/ccs) 14:40 · 18:13 [MIA Miami](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/mia)

Daily · eff **May 21st**

- AA 3759  [Miami MIA](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/mia) 08:55 · 12:15 [CCS Caracas](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/ccs)
- AA 3688  [Caracas CCS](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/ccs) 13:00 · 16:34 [MIA Miami](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/mia)

**The Venezuelan capital will be American Airlines’ 14th destination in South America,** although it would technically be the **15th**, since the **airline also operates flights to Montevideo,** the capital of **Uruguay**, during the **South American summer.**

**American is the leading U.S. airline in South America,** as well as in **Central America**, where it also **has an important route network from/to the United States.** According to our database, these are its services:

# Routes

| Country | City | From/to | Flight(s) | Aircraft |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Anguilla | The Valley | Miami | 14 weekly | Embraer 175 |
| Antigua and Barbuda | St. John | Charlotte | 1 weekly | Boeing 737 |
|  | St. John | Miami | 14 weekly | Boeing 737 |
|  | St. John | New York | 7 weekly | Boeing 737 |
| Argentina | Buenos Aires | Miami | 21 weekly | 777 / 787 |
|  | Buenos Aires | New York | 7 weekly | Boeing 777 |
| Aruba | Oranjestad | Charlotte | 12 weekly | Boeing 737 |
|  | Oranjestad | Dallas | 1 weekly | Airbus A321 |
|  | Oranjestad | Miami | 14 weekly | Airbus A321 |
|  | Oranjestad | New York LGA | 1 weekly | Boeing 737 |
|  | Oranjestad | Philadelphia | 8 weekly | A321 / 737 |
| Barbados | Bridgetown | Charlotte | 5 weekly | Boeing 737 |

PreviousNext

[![](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/American-CCS-2017.jpg)](http://https//www.jetphotos.com/photo/8914523) American Airlines in Caracas – December 2017

# A second carrier?

**Laser Airlines is set to be the second airline to operate flights between Venezuela and the United States,** serving the **same route as American.**

In **May**, the **Venezuelan carrier plans to begin flying to Miami from Caracas using GlobalX’s Airbus A320-200 aircraft,** seating **150 passengers**, the **same aircraft used to transport deportees to Caracas from the US in the past few months.**

**Since Venezuela is listed as IASA Category 2** by the **FAA**, **Venezuelan airlines are not permitted to operate with their own aircraft in the United States**. **Laser** uses its **McDonnell Douglas MD-80s** for **domestic and some regional flights**, and an **Airbus A330** from **Hi Fly** for its **Madrid–Caracas** route.

### Schedule between Miami and Caracas · Laser

Daily

- QL  [Miami MIA](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/mia) 07:30 · 10:45 [CCS Caracas](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/ccs)
- QL  [Caracas CCS](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/ccs) 12:30 · 15:35 [MIA Miami](https://www.flightradar24.com/data/airports/mia)

_Subject to change_

[![](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Global-X.jpg)](https://www.jetphotos.com/photo/12034421)

**Maiquetía International Airport is the country’s main airport,** serving both **domestic and international flights.** Recently, **several foreign airlines have resumed operations in Venezuela,** focusing on **Caracas**.

**Aerocaribe**, **Avior**, **Conviasa**, **Estelar Latinoamérica**, **Rutaca**, **Turpial**, and **Venezolana** are the **Venezuelan airlines currently in operation;** they primarily **operate domestic flights** but also **offer some international routes.** For more information about these airlines and their operations, [you can check out this post we published on the blog.](https://www.flightradar24.com/blog/aviation-news/commercial-aviation-in-venezuela-today/)

# International routes in Venezuela

**Caracas** is **currently connected** to **Barbados, Colombia, Cuba, Curaçao,** the **Dominican Republic, Mexico, Panama, Portugal, Spain,** and **Turkey,** and will be **connected to the United States** starting **April 30th.** Other cities, such as **Barquisimeto**, **Maracaibo**, **Porlamar**, and **Valencia**, have **international connections**, with **all routes within Latin America**.

| From | To | Airline | Flight(s) | Aircraft |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Barquisimeto (BRM) | Panama City (PTY) | Copa | 4 weekly | Boeing 737 |
| Barquisimeto (BRM) | Panama City (PTY) | Rutaca | 2 weekly | Boeing 737 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bogotá (BOG) | avianca | 14 weekly | Airbus A320 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bogotá (BOG) | Avior | 4 weekly | Boeing 737 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bogotá (BOG) | Laser | 7 weekly | MD-82 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bogotá (BOG) | LATAM | 7 weekly | Airbus A320 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bogotá (BOG) | Rutaca | 2 weekly | Boeing 737 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bogotá (BOG) | Wingo | 7 weekly | Boeing 737 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Bridgetown (BGI) | Conviasa | 1 weekly | Embraer 190 |
| Caracas (CCS) | Cancún (CUN) | Conviasa | 1 weekly | Embraer 190 |
